---
title: "Mean differences analysis"
author: "Prerana Chandratre"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r Load libraries and prepare input data}

# Load required libraries
library(openxlsx)  # For reading/writing Excel files
library(dplyr)     # For data manipulation
library(tidyr)     # For data reshaping (not used here but good for pipelines)
library(stringr)   # For string processing

# Define file paths
file_path <- "data/included_studies_1-13.xlsx"       # Path to input Excel file
output_path <- "output/mean_diff.xlsx"               # Path for saving final output

# Read the Excel file, starting from the 2nd row (assumes row 1 contains metadata or notes)
data <- read.xlsx(file_path, startRow = 2)

# Define a custom function to clean messy column names
clean_names <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub('["]', "", x)              # Remove any quote marks
  x <- gsub("&#10;?", "", x)           # Remove HTML line breaks
  x <- gsub("[\r\n]", "", x)           # Remove newline characters
  x <- trimws(x)                       # Remove leading/trailing spaces
  x <- gsub("\\s+", "", x)             # Remove any remaining internal whitespace
  return(x)
}

# Apply the cleaning function to all column names
names(data) <- clean_names(names(data))
```


```{r  Define the static metadata columns that should always be kept in final output}

# These include identifiers and key study-level information
keep_static <- c("0_IDF", "1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "29_WbPoint")

# Find all columns that follow the pattern "_WBmtX" where X is a number (e.g., "_WBmt1", "_WBmt2", ...)
# These are assumed to represent repeated measures over time
wbmt_cols <- grep("_WBmt\\d+$", names(data), value = TRUE)

# Define a helper function to extract the numeric time index from each WBmt column
# For example: from "38_WBmt1" it extracts 1
extract_index <- function(col) {
  as.numeric(str_extract(col, "(?<=_WBmt)\\d+"))  # Use regex to extract the digits after "_WBmt"
}

# Sort the WBmt columns based on their numeric time indices
# This ensures the columns are processed in chronological order
wbmt_cols <- wbmt_cols[order(sapply(wbmt_cols, extract_index))]
```

```{r Compute Difference and Pattern Columns for Consecutive Time Points}

# Initialize empty vectors to store the names of all generated diff and pattern columns
diff_cols <- c()      # To store names like "diff_col1_col2"
pattern_cols <- c()   # To store names like "pattern_col1_col2"

# Loop through each pair of consecutive WBmt columns
for (i in 1:(length(wbmt_cols) - 1)) {
  
  # Get the current column (time t) and the next column (time t+1)
  col1 <- wbmt_cols[i]
  col2 <- wbmt_cols[i + 1]

  # Define names for the new columns: one for difference and one for the pattern
  diff_name <- paste0("diff_", col1, "_", col2)
  pattern_name <- paste0("pattern_", col1, "_", col2)

  # Calculate the difference between time t+1 and time t, and store it in the new column
  data[[diff_name]] <- as.numeric(data[[col2]]) - as.numeric(data[[col1]])

  # Assign a pattern label based on the direction of change:
  # - "I" for increase
  # - "D" for decrease
  # - NA for no change or missing values
  data[[pattern_name]] <- ifelse(is.na(data[[diff_name]]), NA,             # If value is NA, keep NA
                                 ifelse(data[[diff_name]] > 0, "I",        # If positive difference, label "I"
                                        ifelse(data[[diff_name]] < 0, "D", NA)))  # If negative difference, label "D"

  # Save the column names for later use (e.g., for reordering or subsetting)
  diff_cols <- c(diff_cols, diff_name)
  pattern_cols <- c(pattern_cols, pattern_name)
}
```


```{r Reorder Columns and Subset Final Dataset }

# Define the desired column order:
# - Static columns (e.g., ID, Author, Year)
# - Time-based WBmt columns (e.g., repeated measurements)
# - Calculated difference columns (e.g., change scores between timepoints)
# - Calculated pattern columns (e.g., "I", "D", or NA for change direction)
final_col_order <- c(keep_static, wbmt_cols, diff_cols, pattern_cols)

# Subset the original data using only the columns that exist in both the desired list and actual data
# This prevents errors if some expected columns are missing
# drop = FALSE keeps the result as a data frame even if only one column is selected
data_diff_pattern <- data[, intersect(final_col_order, names(data)), drop = FALSE]

```

```{r Combine All Pattern Columns into One Summary Column}

# Identify all columns whose names start with "pattern_"
# This ensures that even if the variable `pattern_cols` was lost, we can still retrieve all relevant columns
pattern_cols <- grep("^pattern_", names(data), value = TRUE)

# Create a new column called 'pattern_summary' that summarizes the entire change pattern for each row
# For each row:
# - Select all pattern values (e.g., "I", "D", NA)
# - Remove any NA values
# - Concatenate the remaining values into a single string (e.g., "IDDI")
data$pattern_summary <- apply(data[, pattern_cols], 1, function(row) {
  paste(na.omit(row), collapse = "")  # Combine non-NA pattern labels into one string per row
})

```

```{r Prepare Final Data Frame with Summary Column for Output or Analysis}

# Define the complete column order:
# - Static study info (e.g., IDs, authorship, year)
# - WBmt timepoint values
# - Computed differences between timepoints
# - Directional pattern labels ("I", "D", NA)
# - Combined pattern summary string across all timepoints
final_col_order <- c(keep_static, wbmt_cols, diff_cols, pattern_cols, "pattern_summary")

# Subset the full dataset to include only those columns that:
# - Match the defined order
# - Actually exist in the current dataset
# 'intersect' ensures we avoid any non-existent columns
# drop = FALSE keeps result as data frame even if only one column is selected
data_diff_pattern <- data[, intersect(final_col_order, names(data)), drop = FALSE]

```

```{r remove repeated columns}

# Define the column names to remove (put all names you want to ignore)
cols_to_ignore <- c(
  "diff_38_WBmt1_diff_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2",
  "diff_diff_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2_pattern_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2",
  "diff_pattern_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2_40_WBmt2",
  "diff_40_WBmt2_diff_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3",
  "diff_diff_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3_pattern_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3",
  "diff_pattern_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3_51_WBmt3",                        
  "diff_51_WBmt3_diff_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4",                            
  "diff_diff_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4_pattern_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4",          
  "diff_pattern_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4_53_WBmt4",                        
  "diff_53_WBmt4_diff_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5",                           
  "diff_diff_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5_pattern_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5",           
  "diff_pattern_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5_64_WBmt5",                         
  "diff_64_WBmt5_diff_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6",                            
  "diff_diff_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6_pattern_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6",           
  "diff_pattern_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6_66_WBmt6",                         
  "diff_66_WBmt6_diff_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7",                            
  "diff_diff_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7_pattern_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7",           
  "diff_pattern_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7_77_WBmt7",                         
  "diff_77_WBmt7_diff_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8",                            
  "diff_diff_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8_pattern_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8",           
  "diff_pattern_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8_79_WBmt8",                         
   "diff_79_WBmt8_diff_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9",                            
  "diff_diff_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9_pattern_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9" ,          
  "diff_pattern_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9_90_WBmt9",                         
  "diff_90_WBmt9_diff_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10",                           
  "diff_diff_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10_pattern_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10",         
  "diff_pattern_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10_92_WBmt10",                       
  "diff_92_WBmt10_diff_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11",                        
  "diff_diff_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11_pattern_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11",     
  "diff_pattern_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11_103_WBmt11",                    
  "diff_103_WBmt11_diff_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12",                      
  "diff_diff_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12_pattern_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12",   
  "diff_pattern_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12_105_WBmt12",                   
  "pattern_38_WBmt1_diff_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2",                         
  "pattern_diff_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2_pattern_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2",        
  "pattern_pattern_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2_40_WBmt2",                      
  "pattern_40_WBmt2_diff_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3",                         
  "pattern_diff_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3_pattern_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3",        
  "pattern_pattern_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3_51_WBmt3",                      
  "pattern_51_WBmt3_diff_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4",                         
  "pattern_diff_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4_pattern_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4",        
  "pattern_pattern_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4_53_WBmt4",                      
  "pattern_53_WBmt4_diff_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5",                         
  "pattern_diff_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5_pattern_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5" ,       
  "pattern_pattern_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5_64_WBmt5",                      
  "pattern_64_WBmt5_diff_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6",                         
  "pattern_diff_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6_pattern_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6",        
  "pattern_pattern_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6_66_WBmt6",                      
  "pattern_66_WBmt6_diff_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7",                         
  "pattern_diff_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7_pattern_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7",        
  "pattern_pattern_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7_77_WBmt7",                      
  "pattern_77_WBmt7_diff_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8",                         
  "pattern_diff_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8_pattern_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8",        
  "pattern_pattern_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8_79_WBmt8",                      
  "pattern_79_WBmt8_diff_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9",                         
  "pattern_diff_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9_pattern_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9",        
  "pattern_pattern_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9_90_WBmt9",                      
  "pattern_90_WBmt9_diff_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10",                        
  "pattern_diff_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10_pattern_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10",      
  "pattern_pattern_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10_92_WBmt10",                    
  "pattern_92_WBmt10_diff_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11",                     
  "pattern_diff_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11_pattern_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11",  
  "pattern_pattern_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11_103_WBmt11",                 
  "pattern_103_WBmt11_diff_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12",                   
  "pattern_diff_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12_pattern_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12",
  "pattern_pattern_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12_105_WBmt12"
)

# Step 2: Drop these columns from your data
data_cleaned <- data_diff_pattern[, setdiff(names(data_diff_pattern), cols_to_ignore), drop = FALSE]
```


```{r Check the cleaned dataset}
print(data_cleaned)
```

```{r Reorder Dataset Using a Manually Defined Column Sequence}

# Define a manual column order to control the structure of the final output explicitly
# This allows for precise control over how timepoints, differences, and patterns are arranged
# The order is based on logical progression from baseline to follow-up assessments
manual_col_order <- c(
  "0_IDF", "1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "29_WbPoint",        # Study metadata
  "38_WBmt1",                                                              # Timepoint 1
  "40_WBmt2", "diff_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2", "pattern_38_WBmt1_40_WBmt2",       # T1→T2
  "51_WBmt3", "diff_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3", "pattern_40_WBmt2_51_WBmt3",       # T2→T3
  "53_WBmt4", "diff_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4", "pattern_51_WBmt3_53_WBmt4",       # T3→T4
  "64_WBmt5", "diff_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5", "pattern_53_WBmt4_64_WBmt5",       # T4→T5
  "66_WBmt6", "diff_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6", "pattern_64_WBmt5_66_WBmt6",       # T5→T6
  "77_WBmt7", "diff_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7", "pattern_66_WBmt6_77_WBmt7",       # T6→T7
  "79_WBmt8", "diff_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8", "pattern_77_WBmt7_79_WBmt8",       # T7→T8
  "90_WBmt9", "diff_77_WBmt8_79_WBmt9", "pattern_79_WBmt8_90_WBmt9",       # T8→T9
  "92_WBmt10", "diff_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10", "pattern_90_WBmt9_92_WBmt10",    # T9→T10
  "103_WBmt11", "diff_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11", "pattern_92_WBmt10_103_WBmt11", # T10→T11
  "105_WBmt12", "diff_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12", "pattern_103_WBmt11_105_WBmt12", # T11→T12
  "pattern_summary"                                                       # Final summarized pattern
)

# Reorder the cleaned dataset using only the columns present in both the manual list and actual data
data_manual <- data_cleaned[, intersect(manual_col_order, names(data_cleaned)), drop = FALSE]

# Replace any empty string values ("") with proper NA for consistency
data_manual[data_manual == ""] <- NA
```



```{r Export Final Processed Data to Excel}

# Write the manually ordered dataset (with diff and pattern columns) to an Excel file
# - Uses the "openxlsx" package
# - Saves the file to the defined path
# - rowNames = FALSE ensures that row numbers are not written as a separate column
write.xlsx(data_manual, "output/mean_diff.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)

# Print a message to the console to confirm that the file was saved successfully
cat(" File with mean differences and patterns saved successfully.\n")

```
